question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a collection of signatures where the iteration order of signatureHashMap.keySet() is non-deterministic (HashMultimap uses a HashMap internally), causing different hash groups to be evaluated in different orders across runs, and in edge cases where multiple groups are near the threshold, producing non-deterministic consensus outcomes that intermittently halt the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the signatures collection to contain duplicate StreamFileSignature objects (same node, same filename), causing their stake to be double-counted in the stake accumulation loop, potentially allowing a minority-stake attacker to falsely satisfy canReachConsensus() and inject a malicious stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature where getNode() returns a ConsensusNode whose getStake() throws an ArithmeticException or StackOverflowError, causing an unhandled exception to propagate out of the inner stake accumulation loop and crash the importer's downloader thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation on the signatures parameter to pass a null Collection reference, causing a NullPointerException at the for-each loop on line 42 (null.iterator()), crashing the validate() call and halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature with a fileHash of zero bytes (all zeros), causing getFileHashAsHex() to return a valid but all-zero hex string that collides with other zero-hash signatures, grouping unrelated signatures under the same hash key and potentially allowing false consensus to be reached with insufficient legitimate stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusCount is incremented by validatedSignatures.size() (not by 1) for each consensus-reaching hash group, causing consensusCount to overflow to a negative value if validatedSignatures.size() is Integer.MAX_VALUE, making the consensusCount > 0 check on line 80 fail and throwing a spurious SignatureVerificationException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature whose getNode() returns a ConsensusNode implementation that performs expensive I/O or database queries inside getStake() or getTotalStake(), causing the validate() method to block indefinitely on each call and starving the importer's thread pool, resulting in a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maximum bound on the number of distinct fileHashAsHex keys in signatureHashMap to inject N unique hashes, causing the second for-loop over signatureHashMap.keySet() to iterate N times with O(N) complexity, consuming excessive CPU and causing a non-network-based computational DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a StreamFileSignature where getNode() returns a ConsensusNode with getTotalStake() == 1 and getStake() == 1, while the actual network has much higher totalStake, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus(1) to compute a threshold of 1, allowing a single-node minority to falsely reach consensus and ingest a malicious stream file?
